core_content.markdown:
### 11. Waring's Problem 

Hint: Observe that if $k\geq1$ and $f(x)$ is integer-valued for all suffilarge x. Represent $f(x)$ $\Delta f(x)$ inthe orm(1)nueinuok.isa 

5. Let that if $f(x)$ be a polynomial of degree k with complex coeficients. Prove $f(x)$ is an integer for al sufficiently large integers x, then $f(x)$ is an integer for all integers x.



6. Prove that if $f(x)$ is an integer-valued polynomial of degree k with leading coeffi  cient $a_{k}$ ,then 



$$|a_{k}|\geq\frac{1}{k!}.$$

7. Let $f(x)$ be an integer-valued polynomial, and define 

$$d=\operatorname*{g c d}\{f(x):x\in\mathbf{N}_{0}\}$$

and 

$$d^{\prime}=\operatorname*{g c d}\{f(x):x\in\mathbf{Z}\}.$$

Let $u_{0},u_{1},\ldots,u_{k}$ be integers such that 

$$f(x)=\sum_{i=0}^{k}u_{i}{\binom{x}{i}}.$$

Prove that 

$$d=d^{\prime}=(u_{0},u_{1},\ldots,u_{k})..1$$

8. Prove that if 

$$f(x)=\sum_{i=0}^{k}u_{i}{\binom{x}{i}},$$

then 

$$f_{1}(x)=f(x+1)=u_{k}\binom{x}{k}+\sum_{i=0}^{k-1}(u_{i}+u_{i+1})\binom{x}{i}.$$

Prove that 

$$\begin{aligned}{}&{{}\operatorname*{g c d}(u_{0},u_{1},\ldots,u_{k-1},u_{k})}\\ {}&{{}\quad=\quad\operatorname*{g c d}(u_{0}+u_{1},u_{1}+u_{2},\ldots,u_{k-1}+u_{k},u_{k}).}\\ \end{aligned}$$

9. Let $f(x)$ be an integer-valued polynomial and let $m\in\mathbf{Z}$ . We define the polynomial $f_{m}(x)=f(x+m)$ . Prove that $f(x)$ and $f_{m}(x)$ are polynomialsof the same degree and with the same leading coeficient.Let $A(f)=\{f(i)\}_{i=0}^{\infty}$ Prove that gcd $(A(f))=\operatorname*{g c d}(A(\stackrel{\smile}{f_{m}}))$
